rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Written Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 4, 2013, Line 4: CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN TERRAZZO CO.'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Written Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful to the court: (1) for both parties to be prepared to discuss their position on whether American Terrazzo Co. is entitled to a jury trial on Paul Ryan Associates' First Amended Cross Complaint; (2) for Paul Ryan Associates to be prepared to explain its position on how American Terrazzo Co. could prepare for trial on the First Amended Cross Complaint without access to the requested attorney billing records during discovery; and (3) for American Terrazzo Co. to be prepared to explain how the causes of action in Paul Ryan Associates' First Amended Cross Complaint can be tried fairly unless they are tried subsequent to a determination of the underlying construction-defect litigation given that Paul Ryan Associates will not know the extent of its damages, if any, attributable to the breaches alleged in the First Amended Cross Complaint until the underlying construction defect litigation is resolved. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE PASCUAL et al VS. JIMMY DIAMOND GLASS COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 4, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANTs JIMMY DIAMOND GLASS, INC., HOA TANG, CHUONG CHAU's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted in part and denied in part. The motion for summary judgment is granted as to the individual defendant Chau. Corporate directors cannot be held vicariously liable for the corporation's torts in which they do not participate. Their liability, if any, stems from their own tortious conduct, not from their status as directors or officers of the enterprise. (Frances T. v. Village Green Owners Assn. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 490, 503.) Plaintiffs do not dispute that Chau is the president and sole owner of Jimmy Diamond Glass Company and was not present at the jobsite and did not provide any supervision of the work performed on the date of the accident. The motion is denied on all other grounds. There is a triable issue of fact regarding whether Defendant Jimmy Diamond Glass Company had the right to control the activities of decedent thereby making decedent a temporary employee for purposes of the worker's compensation law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID REPP VS. VARIETY CLUB OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 4, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT VARIETY CLUB OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Motion for summary adjudication is denied on statute of limitations grounds, and as to the first cause of action; the motion is granted as to the second and third causes of action. Regarding the statute of limitations, Defendant shifted the burden. Plaintiff, however, raises the issue of equitable tolling regarding the period while Plaintiff and Defendants considered resolving the matter, including the mediation proposed by Variety. (Repp Decl. 13.) This defeats summary adjudication on statute of limitations grounds. As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff raises a triable issue as to whether there was a valid enforceable oral contract between the parties. Plaintiff testified at his deposition that he "felt that the written contract was more to verify the previously given oral contract which [defendant] had given me." (Repp Depo. 136:8-10). As to the second cause of action for promissory estoppel, defendant shifted the burden and plaintiff failed to present any facts showing that defendant made a clear and unambiguous promise to induce plaintiff's reliance. Plaintiff volunteered to reduce his hours and salary. (Repp. Depo. 43:20 - 44:22; 133:8-11.) As to the third cause of action, summary adjudication is granted. Defendant shifted the burden, and Plaintiff did not dispute any facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCp § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
